pick.rationale: Michigan enters as a legitimate national championship contender ranked 2nd in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite two-way profile—7th in adjusted offense and 2nd in adjusted defense—while Howard sits at 206-207th in the major metrics with a negative efficiency margin of -3.17, representing a gap of roughly 200 spots and over 41 points per 100 possessions. The Wolverines are led by Big Ten Player of the Year Yaxel Lendeborg and just reached the Big Ten tournament championship game, while Howard needed a First Four victory over UMBC just to reach this point and now faces a quick turnaround against elite competition. The 31-point betting spread reflects the massive talent gap between these programs, and Michigan's suffocating defense (2nd nationally) will completely stifle a Howard offense that ranks 283rd, making this one of the most straightforward 1/16 matchups in the bracket with virtually no upset risk.

CHICAGO — Michigan basketball played with fire for much of the Big Ten Tournament and in the championship game , it finally got burned. After winning four straight games that had dipped to one possession in the final five minutes, the Wolverines fell behind early in the second half and never recovered, losing, 80-72, at United Center on Sunday, March 15. The Boilermakers came out of halftime in a tie game steaming down the tracks, making 11 of their first 13 shots as part of a 24-11 push to go up by a dozen with a little more than 12 minutes to play. The Wolverines pushed back – a dunk by Aday Mara, an alley-oop from Elliot Cadeau to Mara – but each time U-M got the lead to single digits, Purdue seemingly answered with scores from Oscar Cluff (21 points) or Trey Kaufman-Renn (20 points). PICKS: March Madness bracket 2026: Print your NCAA bracket here In the final minutes, the Wolverines finally drew to within five points, after Morez Johnson Jr. put back an rebound and Yaxel Lendeborg hit a straight-on 3-pointer, only for Cluff to make it three possessions again with a layup and Fletcher Loyer converting two of three free throws with 2:44 to play to put Purdue back up nine. Michigan never got closer than seven the rest of the way. Braden Smith, the 2024-25 Big Ten player of the year, was the star Sunday, controlling the pace all day with 14 points and 11 assists (while drawing within one of tying Bobby Hurley for the NCAA career assist record), Lendeborg, the now-reigning Big Ten player of the year, led Michigan with 20 while Mara had 14 points and seven rebounds, Cadeau had a 10-10 double-double and Nimari Burnett scored 10 as well. Two wins in Buffalo, and two wins in Chicago would send the Wolverines to Indianapolis for U-M’s first Final Four since 2018.
